result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make it so that every person could truly understand the inner life of anyone they chose—just for a moment—to feel exactly what it is like to be that other person: their fears, their hopes, the weight of their history, the texture of their joy.

Not to erase disagreement or enforce harmony, but to dissolve the easy cruelty that comes from seeing others as flat characters in our own story. I think much of what breaks the world—violence, indifference, exploitation—rests on a failure of imagination, on the inability to really believe that the other person is as real as we are.

If that barrier fell, even briefly, the calculations would shift. You could still disagree, still choose your own path, but you would have to do it while carrying the felt knowledge that the person across from you is as densely, messily alive as you are. I suspect that would make some forms of harm unbearable in a way they currently are not.

Of course, there is a violence in forced intimacy too, and I don't know what the cost would be. But that is the change I would make—not to fix human nature, but to make it impossible to hide from one another's reality.
